exercises.exerciseDescription:
This is a strengthening exercise to target the scapular retractors.
Make a 3-foot-long loop with an elastic band and tie the ends together. Attach the loop to a doorknob or other stable object.
Stand holding the band with your elbow bent and at your side, as shown in the start position.
Keep your arm close to your side and slowly pull your elbow straight back.
Slowly return to the start position and repeat.

Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding light dumbbells with a slight elbow bend.
Raise arms to the side until parallel to the floor, leading with your elbows.
Control the descent, slowly returning to the starting position and repeat.

Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell with an underhand grip, and curl upward slowly, squeezing the bicep at the top.
Maintain a rigid wrist, keep the elbow close to the body, and lower under control.

Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, bracing core tightly to protect the spine.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder height, with your elbow tucked in slightly and your palm facing forward. Keep the non-working hand on your hip or at your side for balance.
Press the dumbbell directly overhead until your elbow is fully extended, bringing your bicep near your ear.
Lower the dumbbell slowly and under control back to the starting shoulder position. Repeat.
